articles.title: 'I Think the Ergonomics of Generators is Growing on Me'
articles.description: The author notes generator functions have been widely available in JavaScript for a long time, yet “their practicality hasn’t exactly caught on.” This is a great look at what they are and where they can be useful.
articles.author: Alex MacArthur

articles.title: JavaScript's New Superpower? Explicit Resource Management
articles.description: V8 v13.8 introduces the idea of deterministic resource cleanup. Among a range of new symbols and objects for the task, the basic idea is you can wrap any file handle, stream or connection in a using/await using block and the runtime will handle any cleanup once resources go out of scope.
articles.author: Rezvan Mahdavi Hezaveh (V8)

articles.title: Basecoat: shadcn/ui's Magic, But Minus React
articles.description: shadcn/ui is a popular suite of well designed React components, but if you’d like to use them in other contexts too, Basecoat is for you. Basecoat ports the components to a more vanilla form you can use with your framework (or not) of choice.
articles.author: Ronan Berder

articles.title: JavaScript, When (and What) is this?
articles.description: Dives into the complexities of the this keyword, explaining how its value is ultimately determined by the context in which a function is called, rather than where it’s defined. It’s been many years since we’ve covered this elementary topic, but this is a fantastic, fresh two-part take.
articles.author: Mat 'Wilto' Marquis

articles.title: Building a React App with GitHub Copilot
articles.description: A good walkthrough, complete with video, if you prefer, of bringing together several of GitHub Copilot’s features for building a modern JavaScript app quickly.
articles.author: Kedasha Kerr (GitHub)

articles.title: Regular Expressions in JavaScript
articles.description: Powerful but often misunderstood, many will benefit from this roundup of the potential regexes offer to JavaScript developers.
articles.author: Adebayo Adams

articles.title: ANSIS 4.0: ANSI Color Library for Everywhere
articles.description: A library to use the power of ANSI escape sequences for coloring and styling text in many environments including the terminal, Chromium-based browsers, Node, Bun, Deno, and even Next.js. v4.0 is a big upgrade with enough breaking changes to warrant a migration guide for existing users.
articles.author: webdiscus

articles.title: TanStack DB: A Reactive Client Store with Fast Real-Time Sync
articles.description: A new entry to the TanStack family of libraries that extends TanStack Query with collections, live queries and optimistic mutations.
articles.author: TanStack

articles.title: Svelte Sonner: A Toast Notification Component for Svelte
articles.description: You may be familiar with the fantastic Sonner in the React world – now there’s a Svelte version.
articles.author: Robert Soriano

articles.title: tscircuit: Use React for Building Electronics Projects
articles.description: An interesting way to design and lay out electronic circuit boards using a JSX-based approach. GitHub repo.
articles.author: tscircuit Inc.

articles.title: jsdiff 8.0: A JavaScript Text Diffing Implementation
articles.description: Can compare strings for differences in various ways including creating patches. There’s an online demo.
articles.author: Kevin Decker

articles.title: Feedsmith: A Fresh New Web Feed Parser and Generator
articles.description: A new, modern way to both parse and generate RSS, Atom, JSON Feed, OPML, and RDF feeds with support for common namespaces as used in podcast, media, and other niche types of feed.
articles.author: Maciej Lamberski

articles.title: Fx 36.0: A Command-Line JSON Viewer & Processing Tool
articles.description: It's written in Go, but if you’ve got some files full of JSON that you want to slice and dice, Fx is for you (it's easy to install). This week's v36 release adds support for streaming JSON and tailing such streams, it’s also much faster and less memory hungry when parsing large JSON files. A solid update for a handy tool.
articles.author: Anton Medvedev

articles.description: Did you know that console.log supports format strings? I didn't!
The Deno team shares an update on the Fresh framework.
The Angular team is teasing the release of Angular 20 to be on May 29, but we might learn a lot more at Google I/O next week. I'm going to be there, say hi if you see me! :-)
Microsoft announced a round of layoffs this week and, much to the community’s disappointment, Ron Buckton, a key TypeScript contributor, was among those affected.
Brian Clark shares some best practices for creating a modern npm package.
May 14 Security Releases of Node.js – Including v24.0.2 (Current), v23.11.1 (Current), v22.15.1 (LTS), and v20.19.2 (LTS).
Nuxt 3.17 – The full-stack Vue.js-based framework.
Parcel v2.15.0 – The popular build tool gets a new transformer and minifier for HTML and SVG that also supports converting SVGs to JSX for use in React components.
React Router 7.6, MUI X 8.3, Mithril.js 2.3

articles.title: 'MCP is the Coming of Web 2.0 2.0'
articles.description: Anil shares his thoughts on the emerging Model Context Protocol (a.k.a. MCP) — a way for LLMs to interact with apps and third party tools — highlighting its potential to revive an open, interoperable ‘Web 2.0’.
articles.author: Anil Dash

articles.title: The Future of the Web Depends on Getting This Right
articles.description: Mozilla shares its take on the U.S. vs Google search case: “search competition must improve, but this can be done without harming browser competition”.
articles.author: Mozilla

articles.title: Creating Blob Shapes using clip-path: shape()
articles.description: Another cool tutorial from Temani, this time looking at how we can use the shape() function to create gooey, weird blob shapes that you can fill with a background image, and even animate.
articles.author: Temani Afif

articles.title: Accessibility in HTML Emails Remains Critically Under-addressed in 2025
articles.description: The Email Markup Consortium recently released a fresh study on accessibility in HTML emails, looking at over 400,000 emails. The takeaway? Well, there’s disappointment “to see the same issues as we have in previous years”, but a note that “frameworks and drag-and-drop tools are uniquely positioned to raise the accessibility baseline”.
articles.author: Email Markup Consortium

articles.title: ▶  Unshipping: How (And When) to Break Web Features
articles.description: A roughly ~50 minute podcast with Google’s Rick Byers about Blink’s ‘Principles of Web Compatibility’ and a willingness to change and even ‘unship’ web features.
articles.author: Igalia podcast

articles.title: CSS Snippets
articles.description: Jeremy shares a handful of helpful little CSS tidbits and features that he often uses — you may want to drop them into your project too.
articles.author: Jeremy Keith

articles.title: Flipping Layouts When Google Translate Swaps between a Left-to-Right Language and a Right-to-Left Language
articles.description: What’s the right approach here? Chris experiments with presenting his site when translated (to say Arabic) with a direction: rtl; property value set.
articles.author: Chris Coyier

articles.title: ▶  Flexible Wrapping CSS Grid
articles.description: Create a flexible CSS Grid with a varying number of items that expands, shrinks, and wraps to accommodate the number of items that can fit in one grid row. A quick CSS tip well explained in this ~4 minute video.
articles.author: Zoran Jambor

articles.title: "We Launched Our First Shopify Theme"
articles.description: Sure, it’s an update about a new, paid theme, but it’s also an interesting blog post (with a rad design) about what goes into actually building such a thing.
articles.author: Nic Chan

articles.title: Finding the Joy in Alt Text
articles.description: On the fun often found in the added context of alt text.
articles.author: Bryn Newell

articles.title: prompt-kit: A Set of Customizable, High-Quality Components Built for AI Applications
articles.description: The components are built on shadcn/ui with the goal of making it easy to design AI-related interfaces like chat experiences, AI agents, autonomous assistants, and so forth. Repo here.
articles.author: Julien Thibeaut

articles.title: 🎨 AI Color Match: Instantly Match Colors from Any Image
articles.description: This tool lets you upload a source image (for the colors) and an output image. The colors from the source image are applied to the output and you can use a slider for before/after comparisons.
articles.author: Polarr

articles.title: Web Code Tools: Code Generators for Frontend Development
articles.description: Includes generators with live previews for CSS, HTML, JSON-LD, Meta Tags, Open Graph, X Cards, and Robots.txt.
articles.author: Web Code Tools

articles.title: Datastar: The Hypermedia Framework
articles.description: Datastar claims to offer the power of Alpine.js and htmx in one smaller library. It embraces data-* HTML attributes (thus the name), signals, and SSE events, really embracing the hypermedia concept.
articles.author: Star Federation

articles.title: jsPad: A Modern Playground for Writing and Testing JavaScript, TypeScript, HTML, and CSS
articles.description: Same idea as CodePen, JSFiddle, et al, and you can initiate a project via a template from a variety of technologies (JS, TS, React, Tailwind, Vue, Svelte, and even Web Components).
articles.author: jsPad

articles.title: Crosspost: A JavaScript Utility for Posting Across Multiple Social Networks at Once
articles.description: Currently supports eight different services including Bluesky, X, Mastodon, LinkedIn, and others.
articles.author: Nicholas C. Zakas

articles.title: ColorPocket: A Chrome Extension for Organizing and Picking Colors
articles.description: Has a simple set of features that includes an eyedropper, color picker, palette generator, and ability to save colors for later access.
articles.author: Hugo Aracena

articles.title: ▶  Can Windows 95 Browse the Modern Internet in 2025?
articles.description: A quick 10-minute curio. Yes, it’s possible with some noodling, but you’re not gonna have a good time. Unsurprisingly, a usable experience this is not.
articles.author: Eric Parker
